old_claim.note: Pottery provides a capability that enables this technology without being the only possible path.

new_claim_absent: No pottery dependency remains because all 113 candidate whorls in the scoped assemblage are perforated stones rather than ceramic vessel fragments.

source_shape.source_locator: Abstract and assemblage description identify more than one hundred deliberately perforated pebbles as the candidate spindle whorls.
source_shape.source_claim_summary: The evidence-backed whorls are modified stone pebbles whose raw material and manufacture do not involve pottery production.
source_shape.source_support_rationale: Later cultures could reuse potsherds as whorls, but that optional material path is absent from this scoped assemblage.

ontology_before: Spindle-whorl technology was modeled as requiring pottery regardless of artifact material.
ontology_after: The stone-whorl node has no ceramic prerequisite.
invariant_preserved_or_changed: Changed: drop_spindles no longer directly depends on pottery.
would_reject_if: The Nahal Ein-Gev II candidate whorls were reclassified as ceramic rather than stone artifacts.
short_reason: The scoped whorls are perforated stones, not pottery products.
